content.problemStatement:
You are given an N×MN\times MN×M grid, with each cell initially painted white.

You need to paint exactly KKK of these cells black, such that every black cell is reachable from every other black cell, by travelling horizontally, vertically or diagonally through other black cells.

Find the number of ways to paint the grid in such a manner, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case contains a single line of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and KKK — the number of rows, columns in the grid, and the number of cells to be painted black respectively.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways to paint the grid in such a manner, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The summation of N⋅MN\cdot MN⋅M over all testcases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are only 444 valid colorings.
In each valid coloring, exactly one of the 444 corners of the grid is uncolored.
Test case 222: The only valid coloring is to color the whole grid.
